sourceTitle: YouTube vs Blog for Shopify Apps: 12-Month Data
angle: the conversion gap widened every month, it never plateaued
xPost:
We ran YouTube and a blog for the same Shopify app, same audience, 12 months.

Month one, YouTube led 2.5x.
Month twelve, 4.9x.

The blog we had built for years closed the year at 13% of conversions.
YouTube closed at 65%.

Gaps like this do not stay flat. They compound.

sourceTitle: Getting YouTube Views But No Leads? 6 Reasons and How to Fix Each One
angle: read your comments to see who your channel is really for
xPost:
10,000 subscribers. Zero leads.

Read the comments on your last 10 videos.

"Great strategy, I do something similar" means you built an audience of peers.

"Does this work for my industry" means you built an audience of buyers.

Same view count. Different channel.

sourceTitle: YouTube and the B2B Buyer Journey: 82% Choose Day 1 Vendors
angle: the deal is decided before the buyer starts evaluating
xPost:
Google surveyed 2,063 B2B buyers.

82% ended up purchasing from a vendor they already had in mind before research even started.

The deal is decided before anyone opens a comparison spreadsheet.

If you're not already recognized, you're not being evaluated. You're invisible.

sourceTitle: YouTube vs Paid Ads for B2B: Cost-Per-Lead Comparison
angle: true qualified-lead cost of a paid campaign, then it stops
xPost:
You spent $12,000 on LinkedIn Ads last quarter.
94 leads came in.
Sales qualified 11.

That's $1,091 per real opportunity.
Pause the campaign and all of it hits zero.

A video published 18 months ago is still generating leads today.
That's the real comparison.

sourceTitle: YouTube Scriptwriting for B2B Lead Generation: 5-Part Framework
angle: product pitch dies late, move it earlier
xPost:
Your product pitch is dying in the last 30 seconds.

By then 40 to 50 percent of viewers already left.

Move it to the 60 to 70 percent mark instead.

Most of your audience is still there, and you have already earned the right to say it.

Structure decides who hears your offer.

sourceTitle: YouTube Titles for Business: Rank for Buyer Queries
angle: high CTR from wrong audience is worse than low
xPost:
An accounting firm's video hit 9.2 percent CTR.

Looks like a win.

Average view time: 47 seconds. On a 12 minute video.

Wrong clicks told YouTube it was a bad match. Rankings fell within weeks.

High CTR from the wrong buyer is worse than low CTR from the right one.

sourceTitle: How 4 Videos a Month Build a Predictable Pipeline
angle: a tweet dies in minutes, a video library keeps ranking for years
xPost:
An Instagram post is dead in 40 minutes.
A tweet lives for minutes.

Four YouTube videos a month adds up to 48 assets a year that keep ranking.

Even if only ten of them hit, that is ten inbound funnels working while you sleep.

Volume was never the strategy. The library is.

sourceTitle: 7 YouTube Marketing Mistakes That Kill Business Channels
angle: feature demo vs decision video, same product, 9 demos
xPost:
A CRM company posted "5 New Features in Our Spring Release."
800 views. All existing customers.

Next post: "How to Choose a CRM for a 15 Person Agency."
2,400 views. 9 demo requests in one quarter.

Features impress people who already bought.
Decisions attract people about to.

sourceTitle: YouTube Content Strategy: Plan Videos That Convert
angle: the two-filter test for whether a keyword should be a video at all
xPost:
Not every keyword that ranks makes a good video.

Filter one: can you show it on screen. A setup flow or side by side comparison qualifies. A definition does not.

Filter two: does answering it expose a bigger problem your product solves.

Fail both, it belongs on the blog.

sourceTitle: YouTube Marketing Attribution: 5 Signals That Prove Pipeline
angle: 0% software credit vs 53% buyer self-report on same revenue
xPost:
Your dashboard says YouTube generated zero pipeline.

One company tracked $21.5 million in closed revenue.
Software credited video content with 0%.
Buyers, asked directly, said 53%.

Your attribution tool isn't measuring reality.
It's measuring what's easy to click.

sourceTitle: Is vidIQ Worth It? Only If You Want Views, Not Leads
angle: same video scores 90 on views, 40 on buyer intent
xPost:
A video can score 90 out of 100 on a creator SEO checklist.

And under 40 on buyer intent.

Same video, same metadata, two different questions.

One asks: will people watch this.
The other asks: will people buy from this.

Pick the question before you pick a tool.

sourceTitle: YouTube Competitor Analysis: The 5-Point Framework for B2B
angle: small-channel rival beats big-name rival as threat
xPost:
You're watching the wrong competitor.

A 500,000 subscriber channel may rank for none of your buyers' search terms.

A 3,000 subscriber channel that ranks for four of them is quietly taking your customers.

Subscriber count is vanity. Keyword overlap is the real filter.

sourceTitle: How to Find YouTube Autocomplete Keywords (The Right Way)
angle: unsorted keyword lists let weaker competitors win by intent
xPost:
A list of 150 YouTube autocomplete keywords is not a content plan.

Most of it is noise. Comparison keywords convert. How-to keywords build trust. Research barely moves.

Skip sorting by intent and a competitor with less expertise ranks for the exact phrase your buyer typed.

sourceTitle: When YouTube Doesn't Work for Customer Acquisition
angle: the YouTube company that tells 1 in 5 prospects not to
xPost:
We build YouTube channels for a living.

20% of the businesses that contact us hear the opposite: don't start one. Not yet. Maybe not ever.

Impulse buys, tiny markets, 24 hour sales cycles: YouTube can't fix any of those.

Saying no is how we make yes actually work.

sourceTitle: YouTube Search Intent: How to Rank for Buyer Queries in 2026
angle: the four intent types, most channels target the wrong ones
xPost:
YouTube search has four intent types: navigational, informational, commercial, transactional.

Most B2B channels only win informational.

Buyers convert on commercial and transactional queries: comparisons, ready to act.

Wrong intent gets views. Right intent gets pipeline.

sourceTitle: YouTube ROI for SaaS: What a $12k/Year Channel Returns
angle: the ACV divided by 1,000 break-even shortcut most founders skip
xPost:
Divide your annual contract value by 1,000.
That's roughly the YouTube leads you need per year to break even on a $12k channel.

$5,000 ACV: 3 deals.
$50,000 ACV: 1 deal every 4 years.

Most SaaS founders never run this math.
They just assume YouTube is too slow.

sourceTitle: AI Tools for YouTube: What Actually Saves Time (2026)
angle: AI nails structure, humans must supply conviction
xPost:
We tested AI tools for YouTube on a real channel.

Three saved time. Four were marginal. Two made things worse.

AI nails structure. It fakes conviction badly.

Prospects do not buy competent. They buy specific.

Let AI draft it. Let your own stories close the deal.

sourceTitle: The Break-Even Math: How Many Clients Justify YouTube?
angle: run the math twice, once with your time priced honestly
xPost:
Run your YouTube break-even math twice.

Once with your time valued at zero.
Once with your real hourly rate.

10 hours a month at $150 an hour is $18,000 a year, invisible in the cash-only version.

If the channel only pays off when your time is free, it doesn't pay off.

sourceTitle: YouTube Sales Funnel: From First View to Paying Customer
angle: the exact funnel math turning 4 videos into $36k monthly revenue
xPost:
4 videos a month.
1,000 views each.
3% click through to your site.
10% become customers.

That's 12 new customers and $36,000 in monthly revenue.
From content that cost $2,000 to $4,000 to make.

Most channels never do this math. They just count views.

sourceTitle: 10 Best YouTube Rank Checker Tools for Business Channels (2026)
angle: a rank number means nothing without competitor context
xPost:
A rank of number 5 means nothing on its own.

Top 4 with millions of views from huge channels: number 5 is a win.

Top 4 with 2,000 views, three years old: number 5 means you left the top spot on the table.

Check who you are up against, not just your number.
